name: Metropolitan Bank and Trust Company
description_short: Metropolitan Bank and Trust offers international remittance services, wire transfers, letters of credit, and basic deposit products with a focus on overseas money transfer and trade financing.
description_long: Metropolitan Bank and Trust is a for-profit bank operating primarily as a remittance and international trade services provider, with a physical location in New York (phone: 212-832-0855). The company appears to be part of a larger Metrobank group with operations in the Philippines, China, Taiwan, and Japan, suggesting a focus on serving immigrant communities and international business clients. The bank offers traditional deposit products including checking accounts, savings accounts, and certificates of deposit, but emphasizes remittance services (Metroremit) and trade financing as core business lines. Their service menu reveals a specialized banking model centered on money movement rather than comprehensive retail banking. The company operates limited banking hours (Monday-Friday, 9am-3pm only) with a 3pm cutoff for both banking and remittance transactions, reflecting a focused operational model. Metropolitan Bank and Trust provides real-time exchange rate information and maintains multiple international branch relationships, positioning itself as a bridge bank for cross-border transactions and Filipino diaspora banking needs.
services: Metroremit remittance service (with multiple delivery options)
pros: Offers multiple remittance delivery options including door-to-door delivery, mail-in remittance, pickup at agents, and credit to other banks
Provides international wire transfer services for both domestic and international transactions
Specializes in trade services including import/export letters of credit, standby letters of credit, and documentary collections
Displays real-time exchange rates on website (as of stated date), enabling rate transparency
Offers EX-IM Bank guarantees for export financing support
Maintains presence in multiple countries (Philippines, China, Taiwan, Japan) for broader international reach
Provides pension product (Metrobank Superbilis Pension) for retirement planning
cons: Very limited banking hours (Monday-Friday 9am-3pm only; closed weekends) restrict accessibility
Early 3pm cutoff for transactions may be inconvenient for working professionals
Limited information about deposit account features, fees, interest rates, or account minimums on website
No online banking or digital services mentioned despite operating in 2026
Physical presence appears limited to single New York location based on provided information
best_for: Filipino immigrants and diaspora sending money to family in the Philippines
International business owners and importers/exporters requiring trade finance services
Customers seeking door-to-door remittance delivery within New York area
Businesses needing letters of credit and documentary collection services
diagnosis: Metropolitan Bank and Trust is best for Filipino immigrants, international business owners, and customers needing remittance or trade finance services through a bank with cross-border relationships. The primary caveat is extremely limited operating hours (weekdays 9am-3pm only) and early transaction cutoffs that create accessibility barriers for most working customers.
typical_results_timeline: Account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions vary by product.
